name: Molmo 72B
archDisplay: Dense VLM: Qwen2-72B LLM backbone + OpenAI CLIP ViT-L/14-336 vision encoder
note: Pioneered 2D image-pointing; trained on 1M curated PixMo pairs with 1000x less data than peers, rivals GPT-4o/Claude 3.5/Gemini 1.5
tpsNote: 72B model exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at 4-bit (~39GB); needs 48GB+ or multi-GPU

results.notes: Molmo Technical Report ('Molmo and PixMo') Table 1, Molmo-72B row. Column order verified via direct PDF text extraction (pdftotext -raw, which preserved correct left-to-right header order unlike layout mode) and cross-checked against the paper's explicit claims (highest academic average 81.2, second-highest Elo 1077 just behind GPT-4o's 1079 - both match exactly).
Molmo Technical Report Table 1, Molmo-72B row.
VQA v2.0 test-dev; Molmo Technical Report Table 1, Molmo-72B row.
Molmo Technical Report Table 1, Molmo-72B row.

Molmo Technical Report Table 1, Molmo-72B row. Exceeds the taxonomy's documented textvqa range (40-75); this is the value the paper itself reports, corroborated identically by the later Molmo2 Technical Report's own Molmo-72B baseline row.
MMMU val; Molmo Technical Report Table 1, Molmo-72B row.
MathVista testmini; Molmo Technical Report Table 1, Molmo-72B row.

verificationNotes: Existence, Sept 25 2024 release, and Apache-2.0 license confirmed via Ai2 blog/TechCrunch and live HF model card. Both benchmark rows (81.2 academic avg, 1077 human Elo) literally confirmed on the HF card. paramsB=72 matches the official model name; live config.json (hidden=8192, 80 layers) plus safetensors index (293.2GB, fp32) implies actual total weights (LLM backbone + CLIP vision encoder) are closer to ~73.3B, with the Qwen2-72B LLM backbone alone ~72.7B — 72 is the conventional rounded label, kept unchanged. ctxK=4 (4096) confirmed exactly from config.json.
